segments.text: The word of the Lord came to me:
Son of man, how is the wood of a vine different from that of a branch from any of the trees in the forest?
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: Is wood ever taken from it to make anything useful? Do they make pegs from it to hang things on?
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: And after it is thrown on the fire as fuel and the fire burns both ends and chars the middle, is it then useful for anything?
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: If it was not useful for anything when it was whole, how much less can it be made into something useful when the fire has burned it and it is charred?
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: As I have given the wood of the vine among the trees of the forest as fuel for the fire, so will I treat the people living in Jerusalem.
segments.speaker: The Sovereign Lord

segments.text: I will set my face against them. Although they have come out of the fire, the fire will yet consume them. And when I set my face against them, you will know that I am the Lord.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: I will make the land desolate because they have been unfaithful, declares the Sovereign Lord.
segments.speaker: The Sovereign Lord
